2606.09563.title: PRISM: Recovering Instruction Sets from Language Model Activations
2606.09563.paper_summary: As LLMs are deployed as agents, reliable monitoring requires knowing not only what they output, but which instructions are steering their behavior. This is difficult when models infer unintended subgoals, follow contextual cues, or are influenced by prompt injections and hidden objectives. While activation-to-language methods suggest that hidden states can reveal natural-language information, existing approaches are not designed to recover the full set of simultaneous instructions, constraints, prohibitions, and subgoals active in agentic settings. We formalize this problem as instruction set retrieval and introduce PRISM, an activation-conditioned interpreter that decodes hidden states from a frozen target model into a faithful bullet list of active instructions. Unlike prior activation-to-language methods, PRISM is trained to recover instruction sets directly, using judge-guided GRPO to reward covered instructions and penalize unsupported ones. Across benign, constrained, prompt-injection, and hidden-objective settings, PRISM outperforms activation-to-language baselines, especially on security-relevant objectives.
2606.09563.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）被部署為代理，可靠的監控不僅需要了解它們的輸出，還需要知道哪些指令在引導它們的行為。當模型推斷出意想不到的子目標、遵循上下文提示或受到提示注入和隱藏目標的影響時，這變得困難。雖然激活到語言的方法表明隱藏狀態可以揭示自然語言信息，但現有的方法並未設計用來恢復在代理環境中活動的完整指令集、約束、禁止事項和子目標。我們將這個問題形式化為指令集檢索，並介紹PRISM，一種激活條件解釋器，將來自凍結目標模型的隱藏狀態解碼為一個忠實的活動指令子彈列表。與先前的激活到語言方法不同，PRISM被訓練以直接恢復指令集，使用法官引導的GRPO來獎勵已涵蓋的指令並懲罰不支持的指令。在良性、受限、提示注入和隱藏目標的環境中，PRISM的表現超過了激活到語言的基準，特別是在與安全相關的目標上。
2606.09563.author: Gilad Gressel et.al.
2606.09563.authors: Gilad Gressel, Rahul Pankajakshan, Julia Diament, Efim Hudis, Krishnashree Achuthan, Yisroel Mirsky
